title: X (Twitter) Announces Integration with Lightning Network for Bitcoin Payments
summary: X (formerly Twitter) is adding support for Bitcoin payments via the Lightning Network. This move could make it easier for users to send and receive Bitcoin directly on the platform.
body:
X, the social media platform formerly known as Twitter, has announced it will integrate Bitcoin payments using the Lightning Network. This feature will allow users to send and receive Bitcoin directly through the platform, potentially streamlining transactions and reducing fees.

The Lightning Network is a layer-two solution built on top of the Bitcoin blockchain, designed to enable faster and cheaper transactions. This integration could make Bitcoin more accessible to X's large user base, which includes both crypto enthusiasts and newcomers.

For everyday users, this means more options for sending and receiving money globally without relying on traditional banking systems. It could also drive further adoption of Bitcoin as a medium of exchange, making it more relevant in daily transactions.

Users should watch for the official rollout of this feature, as it could significantly impact how Bitcoin is used on social media platforms. Keep an eye on X's announcements for updates on when this integration will be available to all users.
twitterThread: X (Twitter) is bringing Bitcoin payments to its platform via the Lightning Network. Faster, cheaper transactions could be coming soon. 🚀
The Lightning Network is a layer-two solution that speeds up Bitcoin transactions and reduces fees. This integration could make Bitcoin more accessible to millions of users.

Keep an eye on X's announcements for the official rollout. This could change how we use Bitcoin on social media. Read more → https://www.cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-06-22-x-twitter-announces-integration-with-lightning-network-for-bitcoin-payments
